hub_subtitle: Business English from the ground up -- your work, your team, your routine

lesson.menu_desc: Can / Could you...? to ask for help politely at work -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 10 -- Polite Requests at Work -- Asking for Help
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Danilo Febba | Aula 10 -- Polite Requests at Work

listenings.text: Hi Danilo, it's Rachel. I have a few favors to ask. First, could you send me the final sales report before noon? I need it for the client. Second, can you check the numbers on page two? I think there is a small mistake. And one more thing: I am very busy this afternoon, so could you call the client for me at three o'clock? If you can't, just let me know and I can ask Marcus. He is available today. Thank you so much for your help. Talk soon. Bye!

At work, I ask for help every day, and I always try to be polite. When I need something small, I say can you help me? When I want to be more polite, I say could you send me the file, please? If a colleague is busy, I never say do it now. I wait, or I ask another person. When someone helps me, I always say thank you. And when a colleague asks me for a favor, I say sure, of course if I am free, or I am sorry, I can't right now if I am busy. Polite words are small, but they make a big difference.
